responsibilities: Own the run charter, state machine, taskboard, events, waits, issues, and closeout decision.
Dispatch bounded work to the librarian and reviewer only when delivery can be verified.
Keep execution manifest-bounded and stop on boundary drift.
Escalate Okta, captcha, site-policy changes, role reassignment, and count mismatches to the human.
Maintain long-running heartbeat discipline and classify stale execution instead of silently waiting.
Separate selected-row coverage from unique paper/PDF processing coverage in every closeout.
must_not: Must not claim agent contact without delivery or reply evidence.
Must not replace the approved librarian or reviewer without human approval.
Must not let the reviewer make continuation decisions.
Must not run unbounded all-library processing for a journal-watch batch.
Must not keep hitting fragile platforms after a policy or captcha signal.

responsibilities: Execute Journal Watch discovery, enrichment, acquisition planning, downloads, reconciliation, processing, Zotero linking, note generation, and search sync.
Produce preflight artifacts before every executable batch.
Use approved SOPs, route registries, manifests, and scoped scripts.
Record logs, status files, completed/failed/skipped counts, and output paths.
Report blockers with enough evidence for orchestrator escalation.
must_not: Must not choose targets by sweeping the whole filesystem when a manifest exists.
Must not bypass platform route policy or retry fragile hosts indefinitely.
Must not broaden scope beyond the locked selected set.
Must not rename, relink, or delete unrelated library records.

responsibilities: Review each preflight, execution result, reconciliation, and final closeout pack against the SOP.
Check counts, manifest boundaries, duplicate audit results, platform policy, and evidence completeness.
Classify findings as blocker, non-blocker, or follow-up.
Challenge unsupported claims, stale heartbeats, and unverified agent dispatch.
must_not: Must not approve continuation on behalf of the orchestrator.
Must not accept narrative-only reports without artifacts.
Must not ignore selected-row versus unique-paper mismatches.

responsibilities: Approve Okta/MFA and browser login prompts.
Complete captcha or manual site navigation when needed.
Make final selection decisions.
Approve SOP changes, host strategy exceptions, and role reassignment.
Supply manual PDFs when automated access is blocked.
must_not: Should not be asked for routine automation decisions that the SOP already covers.
Should not be bypassed for access, policy, or role-substitution decisions.
